frameworks.name: psychophysical threshold estimation
frameworks.one_line: discrimination thresholds index timing precision in auditory tasks

frameworks.name: dissociable timing mechanisms (milliseconds vs seconds)
frameworks.one_line: separate substrates posited for sub- and supra-second interval timing

frameworks.name: pure timing deficit hypothesis
frameworks.one_line: ADHD impairment localized to timing rather than cognitive control

core_question: Are auditory time thresholds in the millisecond and second ranges differentially impaired in ADHD?
key_assumptions: millisecond timing loads less on cognitive control and working memory than seconds-range timing
millisecond and seconds-range timing rely on partially separate mechanisms
non-verbal IQ can be regressed out as a covariate
manipulations: duration range (milliseconds 0.25-1 s vs seconds 0.75-3 s)
key_findings: severe ADHD impairment for millisecond thresholds
no group difference for seconds-range thresholds
millisecond deficit persists after regressing out non-verbal IQ
millisecond and seconds thresholds are uncorrelated
interpretations: partially separate mechanisms for millisecond and seconds timing
ADHD shows a pure timing deficit at short intervals, independent of cognitive control load
